exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Consistent and explicit focus on the prescribed title throughout the essay
Clear, coherent arguments supported by a range of relevant examples from both natural and human sciences
Awareness and evaluation of different points of view, including claims and counterclaims
Strong concluding insight that learning opens new mysteries, demonstrating engagement with wider implications
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally lapses into descriptive summary rather than sustained critical evaluation
Treatment of psychological examples lacks depth in interrogating cultural or contextual factors
References and citations are inconsistently formatted and rely on general sources rather than primary research
Structural coherence could be improved by a clearer thesis framing and more precise operational definitions of key concepts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment places this essay solidly in the 7â8 band, highlighting its sustained focus, clear argumentation, and effective use of examples. However, noted inconsistencies in depth of analysis, occasional descriptiveness, and underdeveloped TOK concepts justify positioning at the lower end of that band. Given the official examiner total of 6, this score reflects both the high points of engagement and coherence and the areas requiring further critical rigour and structural refinement.

annotations.comment: The identification of natural science and social science as the two AOKs is clear and appropriate, setting a solid foundation for comparative analysis.
The terminology is inconsistent: the essay refers to âsocial scienceâ here but âhuman sciencesâ elsewhere. Standardize your AOK labels to avoid confusion.
The student has chosen the prescribed title explicitly and clearly presented the essay focus, demonstrating correct title selection and direct engagement with the knowledge question.
Good methodological insight: you correctly identify reason as a way of knowing that can transform wonder into organized facts, demonstrating ToK awareness.
Definitions of âacquiring knowledgeâ and âsense of wonderâ are overly broad and lack reference to ToK concepts such as ways of knowing or the knowledge framework.
The operational definition of sense of wonder remains vague; consider linking it to specific emotions or imagination as ways of knowing to strengthen your analysis.
The contrast between natural scienceâs predictability and psychologyâs complexity insightfully highlights differences in how wonder is addressed across AOKs.
The neuroplasticity example is wellâchosen, demonstrating how insights into human adaptability can reignite wonder and counter the deterministic view.
Strong final insight: acknowledging that learning opens new mysteries maintains sustained focus on the title and highlights broader implications.
This paragraph would benefit from linking neuroplasticity to additional ways of knowing (e.g., emotion, memory) to deepen the ToK analysis.
Your thesis statement outlines the essayâs aims, but it could be more precise by framing a clear knowledge question and previewing the structure of claims and counterclaims.
The citation for photosynthesis is drawn from a general encyclopedia; incorporate a primary scientific source or peerâreviewed study to strengthen academic rigor.
This paragraph repeats the CRISPRâCas9 discussion with similar points; consolidate these ideas to improve concision and coherence.
The opening could include a concise structural overview of the essayâs line of argumentation to guide the reader and improve coherence.
The photosynthesis example is descriptive but does not explicitly show how mechanistic explanations via reason diminish wonder; link it back to the feeling of awe more directly.
An empty list entry appears at the end of the references (â-â); remove or complete this entry to avoid the appearance of an oversight.
The CRISPRâCas9 example is a compelling counterclaim that shows how advanced knowledge can renew wonder by revealing deeper mysteries.
Relying on a dictionary definition for CRISPRâCas9 limits depth; reference a journal article or seminal research to lend more credibility to your example.
Good awareness of ethical and philosophical dimensions, showing how scope questions generate new lines of inquiry and deepen the sense of wonder.
The analysis privileges scope; consider integrating reliability or ethical ways of knowing to enrich the knowledge framework discussion.
The application of confirmation bias and Dunning-Kruger with realâworld examples grounds abstract theory effectively and enhances reader engagement.
Linking news consumption patterns to confirmation bias brings immediacy to the discussion, illustrating how knowledge shapes perception in daily life.
The psychological biases section is largely descriptive; deepen evaluation by examining how context or culture might alter these effects on wonder.
Critique of Maslowâs hierarchy omits wellâknown cultural and empirical criticisms; evaluating these limitations would strengthen your counterclaim.
The phrase âtransform itâ in reference to wonder is vague; clarify precisely how wonder is reshaped by acquiring knowledge to avoid ambiguity.
The bibliography heading is followed by inconsistent citation formats; apply a uniform style (e.g., APA or MLA) for all sources.
The sentence beginning âTherefore, when we rely too much on theoreticalâ is incomplete, interrupting the flow. Revise to complete your thought and improve clarity.
The conclusion restates your stance but lacks explicit reference to implications for knowledge claims or formulation of a concluding knowledge question.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
